items.title: In pictures: Alice Springs celebrates 30 years of unique craft festival

children.content: As the Alice Springs Beanie Festival celebrates its 30th and final year, we're exploring the weird and wonderful handmade creations — and the characters working hard behind the scenes — that have made the annual event so special.

gallery.altText: A man smiling at the camera, on his head is a handmade beanie featuring a knitted monkey holding a banana.
A group of women wearing colourful knit beanies, smiling as they take a photo together.
A boy holds a colourful knitted fish while wearing a handmade beanie, which has small yellow creature stitched on the top.

thumbnail.altText: A man smiling at the camera, on his head is a handmade beanie featuring a knitted monkey holding a banana.
A group of women wearing colourful knit beanies, smiling as they take a photo together.
A boy holds a colourful knitted fish while wearing a handmade beanie, which has small yellow creature stitched on the top.

avatar.alt: Victoria Ellis, a brunette, curly haired woman leans one elbow on a fence and smiles at the camera.

items.title: NT government reveals scale of bungled smart meter rollout

children.content: The Northern Territory's main energy retailer, Jacana, reveals about 5,000 customers — thousands more than previously thought — have been caught up in a smart meter bungle leading to long delays in their billing.

image.altText: A smart meter

items.title: Couple forced to live on verandah for three months awaiting flood repairs

children.content: Katherine couple Nicki and Luke Asling have been living on their back verandah for the past three months, after their home was flooded to the ceiling in March. Local renovators say it will take months for all the repairs in town to be completed.

image.altText: A woman sits on a bed outdoors, patting a dog.

items.title: Call for youth foyer to help tackle homelessness in the NT

children.content: Youth homelessness rates remain stubbornly high in Central Australia and advocates are renewing calls for a transitional housing model for young people to be established to tackle the issue.

gallery.altText: a pile of pillows on a sandy ground with bushland in the background

thumbnail.altText: a pile of pillows on a sandy ground with bushland in the background

items.title: Legal aid worker caught in bed with fugitive teen killer pleads guilty

children.content: A 37-year-old legal aid worker who was arrested while sharing a hotel bed with a teenage killer she helped evade authorities after he breached his parole has pleaded guilty to attempting to pervert the course of justice.

image.altText: A tracking bracelet on a man's ankle.

items.title: NT's new climate resilience plan lists gas project as top priority

children.content: The Northern Territory government's newly released climate resilience plan has listed the acceleration of gas extraction at the Beetaloo Basin as a top priority. Environment groups have fiercely criticised the plan, saying it will "make climate change worse".

image.altText: An aerial view of a fracking exploration site in the Beetaloo Basin.

items.title: Men tear gassed in Darwin's youth prison have payout slashed by High Court

children.content: The High Court slashes an award for exemplary damages for four former inmates from Darwin's notorious Don Dale Youth Detention Centre who were affected by a tear gas incident in 2014.

gallery.altText: The exterior of the Don Dale Youth Detention Centre, with barbed wire in the foreground.

thumbnail.altText: The exterior of the Don Dale Youth Detention Centre, with barbed wire in the foreground.

items.title: Unions and Inpex reach deal after weeks of strikes and negotiation

children.content: Workers at the Northern Territory's Inpex Ichthys facility have restarted regular duties after unions and the Japanese-owned company struck a deal to end a dispute that threatened to disrupt LNG exports.

image.altText: Inpex's Ichthys LNG Plant is on Darwin Harbour.

items.title: Women forced to labour in tearoom, run-down wards at NT's busiest hospital

children.content: From a mother forced to labour in a hospital's tearoom, to a baby sent home with jaundice on a busy night, doctors warn they are no longer able to provide NT patients with the standard of care they deserve.

image.altText: A woman with dark hair, holding a baby who is facing away while she looks right at the camera.

items.title: Hanson's 'scary' parental leave views outdated, advocates say

children.content: Pauline Hanson's openness to discuss a winding back of paid parental leave is concerning and harks back to an Australia that no longer exists, advocates say.

articles.title: An orange bulldozer is ploughing through the political arena

children.content: Four years ago, Australians decided they didn't want a bulldozer for a prime minister. But now if Gina Rinehart has her way, a big orange one will be ploughing her way through the political arena.

image.altText: pauline hanson gina rinehart bulldozer

articles.title: The 2 minutes and 46 seconds of Pauline Hanson's speech that she thinks matters

children.content: When Pauline Hanson stood up at the National Press Club, it was not just for the audience watching on broadcast TV. In the age of social media, the bite-sized clips that come later are far more important.

image.altText: A woman wearing a black and white dress behind a lectern in colour. Background is social media posts in black and white

gallery.altText: A woman with two children on a couch.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with two children on a couch.
A red-haired woman in glasses and a houndstooth jacket — Pauline Hanson — stands at a lectern.

items.title: Treasurer defends budget backdown despite Labor losing 'political paint'

children.content: Treasurer Jim Chalmers says the federal government has lost "political paint" from May's budget while defending a backdown on key budget measures. Follow live.

image.altText: Jim Chalmers in a grey suit

items.title: Virgin extends travel date for expiring COVID flight credits after criticism

children.content: Customers will still have to spend their COVID-19 flight credits credits by June 30, but will now have more time to travel.

image.altText: The tail of an airliner with "Virgin" written on it in red.

images.altText: Ismael Kone is stretchered off by FIFA medical staff after suffering an injury
Cyle Larin screams in delight in celebration of a goal
Johan Manzambi celebrates his goal with teammate Denis Zakaria

articles.title: The 'mental toughness' that created an Aussie World Cup 'superstar'

children.content: At just 22, Patrick Beach stunned the world in his FIFA World Cup debut and third international start with the Socceroos against Türkiye last weekend.

image.altText: A composite of Beach in Socceroos uniform pumping his fist in celebration next to him as a child playing soccer.

articles.title: Messi calls for 'humanity' as father receives treatment

children.content: Argentina superstar Lionel Messi and his family ask the media for "humanity" amid rumours about his father Jorge Messi's health.

image.altText: Lionel Messi looks emotional during a game for Argentina

articles.title: Your full World Cup schedule in Australian times

children.content: Check out when your team's matches are kicking off at the 2026 FIFA World Cup so you don't miss a match.

image.altText: Generic 2026 FIFA World Cup football

children.content: Canada is on track for its first ever win at a World Cup, but celebrations against Qatar have been soured by a broken leg suffered by Ismael Kone. Follow live.

items.title: Horrific injury mars Canadian celebrations against Qatar
Kyle Sandilands reveals new show, plus talks with One Nation leader

children.content: Radio shock jock Kyle Sandilands reveals the details of his next move after accepting a "piddly little" settlement of $12 million in his contract dispute with ARN Media.

image.altText: Kyle Sandilands speaks into a gold-coloured microphone in a radio studio.

items.title: The pros and cons of retiring overseas with your pension

children.content: With some foreign countries offering cheaper living expenses, it is no surprise that moving overseas to retire is increasingly attractive to some older Australians. But before you up stumps and book that flight, read this.

image.altText: Older man looks out onto a beach.

items.title: More soft plastics needed as Australia's recycling capacity booms

children.content: Australia's soft plastics recycling industry is now operating at nearly triple the capacity it was at REDcycle's peak, but consumers are yet to be convinced.

gallery.altText: A close up of shredded recycled soft plastic.
A hand holding a bag full of soft plastics.
A wide shot of a soft plastics recycling facility, showing conveyer belts.

thumbnail.altText: A close up of shredded recycled soft plastic.
A hand holding a bag full of soft plastics.
A wide shot of a soft plastics recycling facility, showing conveyer belts.

items.title: Crows legend Tony Modra seriously injured in crash

children.content: Crows legend Tony Modra has been seriously injured in a truck crash.

image.altText: A man wearing a black shirt smiles.

items.title: New data shines light on state of EV public charger network

children.content: It's often claimed Australia has an EV public charger shortage, but analysis of exclusive data shows most fast charger sites are not congested.

image.altText: EV charging site with empty bays.

items.title: Would you live in a 1930s pump station? This couple made one their home

children.content: From former post offices to pumping stations, there are buildings with commercial histories all around Australia. Could repurposing them into homes help with the housing supply?

gallery.altText: An old, small building with a rusted tin roof sits in front of a river.
Small and medium rise buildings on the edge of a city

thumbnail.altText: A large red brick building.
An old, small building with a rusted tin roof sits in front of a river.
Small and medium rise buildings on the edge of a city

items.title: Friday news quiz: Soccer celebrations, bin battles, phone fails and an internet-famous duck

children.content: Today's news quiz is heating up like El Niño. Will you strut like an internet-famous duck or get schooled like Prince George?

image.altText: A duck pictured wearing a Mexican football jersey

items.title: No princesses, no musical numbers: How Toy Story changed children's cinema

children.content: The multi-billion-dollar franchise has overcome rewrites, accidental deletions and generations of changing tastes to bring Toy Story 5 to cinemas in 2026.

image.altText: (L-R) Buzz voice actor Tim Allen, John Lasseter and Woody voice actor Tom Hanks promoting Toy Story 2 in 1999.

items.title: 'Sleepwalking into a catastrophe': Proposed data centre raises water fears

children.content: To its supporters, this proposed data centre will provide much-needed regional jobs, but in the nation's driest state, critics say the draw on river water to cool the machines could be too great.

gallery.altText: A brightly coloured model of a data centre.

thumbnail.altText: A brightly coloured model of a data centre.

items.title: Man arrested after boy critically injured in crocodile enclosure at UK zoo

children.content: British police say they have arrested a man on suspicion of attempted murder after being called to a popular zoo north of London.

image.altText: A large crocodile in an enclosure

items.title: Inquest to probe Adelaide fashion icon's death after using magic mushrooms

children.content: The death of an Adelaide fashion industry icon who suffered a cardiac arrest after taking magic mushrooms as an alternative treatment will be probed at a coronial inquest.

image.altText: A woman in a white shirt is photographed outdoors with soft sunlight and greenery in the background.

items.title: Family changes farm to keep Olive at home after dementia diagnosis

children.content: After Olive Eather was diagnosed with dementia, the family farmstay changed its business model to keep her at home for longer.

image.altText: An elderly woman sitting at a table smiles, she is wearing glasses

items.title: Bruce Lehrmann makes legal bid for Brittany Higgins documentary footage

children.content: Former Liberal staffer Bruce Lehrmann has asked a court to order the company behind a film involving Brittany Higgins to hand over raw footage.

image.altText: A man in a suit and tie

items.title: Democrats chewing nails as Trump targets controversial Senate pick

children.content: A Democrat is accused of being a Republican in disguise; a Republican is accused of being a Democrat plant. It's primaries season in the US, and suspicions of dirty pool are rife.

gallery.altText: Platner raises one hand in a wave at a campaign rally

thumbnail.altText: Platner raises one hand in a wave at a campaign rally

items.title: Migration dips as Coalition accuses government of failing to meet targets

children.content: Net overseas migration remained above 300,000 last year, fuelling opposition claims Labor has locked in an unsustainable new normal despite the number of arrivals continuing to fall.

image.altText: A crowd pulses through the city

items.title: Hegseth blasts NATO members, announces review of US forces in Europe

children.content: He said that too many NATO allies failed to support the US on Iran in what he called a "shameful" denial of access, basing and overflight.

image.altText: Pete Hegseth speaks during a press statement on arrival for a meeting of NATO defense ministers

items.title: 'Quite emotional': Renowned artist's tapestries on display after 50 years

children.content: Tapestries depicting the life of Saint Francis of Assisi will be displayed together for the first time since being commissioned in the late 1960s by Australian artist Arthur Boyd.

gallery.altText: A large tapestry depicting a man laying on the ground as another man beats him with a stick.
A large tapestry depicting a man laying his hands on another person to heal them.
A black and white image of an older man with white hair standing in a painting studio.

items.title: Fire authorities failed to meet targets ahead of 2024 Grampians blazes

children.content: An auditor-general's report finds fire authorities missed planned fuel reduction targets before two disastrous fire seasons and warns properties could be at risk if strategic fuel breaks are not put in place.

image.altText: A crew of firefighters in full protective gear and high-visibility shirts look out onto the tarmac of an airport.

items.title: Gina Rinehart says Qld should give some of its islands to Elon Musk

children.content: The mining magnate, speaking at the National Bush Summit, also talked about how she believed Townsville could become a base for the Israeli military.

image.altText: Woman on stage

items.title: Auditors unsure when Snowy 2.0 will be finished

children.content: An audit into the mega hydro-electric project in the NSW Snowy Mountains has outlined five recommendations.

image.altText: Two men in construction outfits, standing in a tunnel.

items.title: Ancient oak tree linked to the legend of Robin Hood is dead

children.content: The tree is said to have sheltered Robin Hood, the legendary 13th-century bandit who stole from the rich and gave to the poor and took refuge in Nottingham Forest.

image.altText: An upwards view of tree trunks.

items.title: Push to turn NSW south coast into state's largest national park

children.content: The National Parks Association wants an ambitious 1.2-million-hectare national park spanning from the Illawarra to the Victorian border.
